steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: Paredes's core argument is that Mexican American folklore results from “ongoing interactions” of different cultures. “Ongoing” implies continuous, present-day mixing—not merely inherited from Spain's colonial past.

steps.title: Match the finding to the theory
steps.content:
Choice D — Most of the folklore that the ethnographers collected consisted of corridos —ballads about history and social life—of a clearly recent origin. directly supports this. Corridos of “clearly recent origin” show folklore being actively created recently—the hallmark of ongoing cultural interactions rather than colonial inheritance.

The other choices either highlight Spanish heritage or do not demonstrate recent creation.

That matches Choice D — Most of the folklore that the ethnographers collected consisted of corridos —ballads about history and social life—of a clearly recent origin..
